  <investigated>Four gaps in V3 specification implementation were identified and addressed: absoluteAnchor extraction logic (§14.1), merged cell header extraction (§9), image-to-merged-range cross-referencing (§9), and missing description field alignment</investigated>
  
  <learned>The conversion tool now extracts three types of image anchors (twoCell, oneCell, and absoluteAnchor), with a new `_anchor_cell()` helper that marks absolute anchors explicitly. Merged cells can have their header values read via `data_only` mode. Image positions can be cross-referenced with merged cell ranges to annotate which images overlay merged regions. The test file contains 75 images and 1074 merged cells, with 521 merged cells having headers and 29 images overlaying merged ranges.</learned>
  
  <completed>Modified `convert.py` with new extraction logic for absoluteAnchor images, updated `get_merged_cells` to extract header values, added `_annotate_merged_range()` function for image-merge cross-referencing, and added null description field. Updated `README.md` and `CLAUDE.md` to document the new metadata schema fields. All changes verified via py_compile, real .xlsx execution, and metadata field validation.</completed>
  
  <next_steps>Three follow-up items flagged: (1) §7.4 merged header propagation to .md tables intentionally skipped pending user decision, (2) pre-existing lint warning at convert.py:288 (f-string without placeholder) left untouched per convention, (3) example output in `output/` directory still uses old schema and needs regeneration with original .xlsx file. Awaiting user input on whether to clean up lint warning and/or regenerate example outputs.</next_steps>
  
  <notes>Changes are additive and backward-compatible for downstream agents. The implementation deliberately avoided §7.4 (merged header markdown table propagation) based on earlier pushback, keeping metadata-level exposure only. User asked about regeneration workflow for the spec file, suggesting ongoing process refinement.</notes>
</summary>
